product_direction: A VS Code extension that opens Markdown files in the browser or a preview panel via right-click, with rich rendering (Mermaid, syntax highlighting, GitHub alerts, emoji, themes).

features.title: Create project harness
features.description: Initialize the harness engineering scaffold and work-control memory.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, init.sh, and .claude/settings.json exist with the chosen autonomy rules and checkpoint hooks.
harness/feature_list.json tracks the implementation queue.
harness/progress.md records initial decisions.
docs/adr/0001-harness-init.md records the harness profile.

features.title: Core open-in-browser / open-in-preview commands
features.description: Right-click context menu (Explorer, editor, command palette) opens .md files as styled HTML in the default browser or in a VS Code preview panel.
acceptance_criteria: openmd.openBrowser and openmd.openPreview commands are registered and appear in context menus for .md files.
Browser open produces a GitHub-style HTML rendering of the Markdown file.

features.title: Rich Markdown rendering
features.description: markdown-it pipeline with Mermaid diagrams, highlight.js syntax highlighting, GitHub alerts, emoji shortcodes, task lists, anchor links, and copy-code buttons (v0.1.0–0.1.4).
acceptance_criteria: Mermaid code fences render as diagrams after page load.
Code blocks are syntax-highlighted and have a working copy button.
[!NOTE]/[!TIP]/[!WARNING]/[!CAUTION]/[!IMPORTANT] alerts render with styling.
:emoji: shortcodes convert to emoji.

features.title: Unified theme system
features.description: Light/Dark/Auto theme toggle in browser preview and VS Code-theme/Light/Dark toggle in the preview panel, sharing one CSS/theme logic with persisted preference (v0.0.5–0.0.7).
acceptance_criteria: Theme toggle works in both browser and preview panel.
Preference persists across reloads via localStorage.

features.title: v1.0.0: Mirror-path temp files and global cleanup
features.description: Temp HTML files are created mirroring the source file's path (e.g. Project/OpenMD/README.md → .temp/Project/OpenMD/README.html) and all temp files from every extension version are cleaned up on activation. Code changes exist uncommitted; openmd-1.0.0.vsix is built.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a Markdown file in the browser writes the temp HTML under a mirror of the source path.
Activating the extension removes leftover temp files from all prior versions.
CHANGELOG.md documents v1.0.0 and package.json version is 1.0.0.
Changes are committed and the v1.0.0 vsix is published (publish requires human approval).

features.title: Automated test suite
features.description: Expand unit tests to cover the full render pipeline (~25 tests) and verify the legacy integration suite runs. Spec: docs/specs/OMD-006-unit-test-expansion.md.
features.status_note: Render-feature unit expansion and title-escaping fix implemented; host integration verification is pending.
acceptance_criteria: npm run test:unit passes with ~25 tests covering task lists, anchors, attrs, emoji, all alert types, theme toggle, copy button, and title escaping.
npm test (legacy integration suite) runs successfully on the host.

features.title: Bundle with esbuild + full offline support
features.description: Bundle extension code with esbuild (vsix ~24 MB → ~2-3 MB), ship mermaid/highlight.js assets locally instead of CDN, and unify the two duplicate HTML templates into one generateHtml(mode). Spec: docs/specs/OMD-008-bundle-offline.md.
acceptance_criteria: vsce package produces a vsix ≤ 3 MB.
With networking disabled, both commands render Mermaid and highlighted code correctly.
No http/https URLs remain in generated HTML.
Single generateHtml(content, fileUri, mode) replaces both duplicate templates.

features.title: Auto-refresh on save (preview panel + browser)
features.description: Preview panel re-renders on onDidSaveTextDocument; browser preview served from a minimal localhost HTTP server (Node built-in http, no new deps) with 1s mtime polling and scroll preservation. Spec: docs/specs/OMD-009-auto-refresh.md.
features.status_note: Implementation and packaging complete; manual F5 smoke test is pending, so this remains in_progress.
acceptance_criteria: Saving a previewed file updates the panel instantly and reloads the browser tab within 2 s with scroll preserved.
Server binds to 127.0.0.1 only, uses an OS-assigned port, and stops on deactivate.
If the server cannot start, falls back to the v1.0.0 temp-file behavior with a warning.

features.title: Keep only the latest vsix in the repo
features.description: Track exactly one vsix (current release) via .gitignore negation; untrack the six older ones; document the release convention for agents. Spec: docs/specs/OMD-007-slim-repo.md.
acceptance_criteria: git ls-files '*.vsix' and ls *.vsix both list exactly the current release vsix (older files deleted from disk).
.gitignore contains *.vsix with a negation for the current release.
CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and harness/progress.md document the convention.

features.title: Cache-Control: no-store on preview server dynamic responses
features.description: Send Cache-Control: no-store on rendered HTML pages and /mtime JSON so browsers never serve stale previews; /assets/ unchanged. Spec: docs/specs/OMD-010-no-store-cache-headers.md.
acceptance_criteria: HTML page responses include Cache-Control: no-store.
/mtime responses include Cache-Control: no-store.
/assets/ responses have no Cache-Control header.
Unit tests assert all three; npm run compile and full test suite pass.

features.title: CI release pipeline (GitHub Actions publish on tag)
features.description: GitHub Actions workflow publishes to VS Code Marketplace + Open VSX + GitHub Release on v* tag push, using repo secrets VSCE_PAT/OVSX_TOKEN. Actions pinned to commit SHAs. Token how-to documented in docs/RELEASE.md.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/release.yml triggers on v* tags, runs unit tests, publishes to both stores via repo secrets.
Third-party actions pinned to commit SHAs.
docs/RELEASE.md documents the release flow and how to (re)issue both tokens.
A real tag push publishes successfully end-to-end (verified with v1.2.1).

features.title: Math rendering (KaTeX) + footnotes — offline, server-side
features.description: Server-side KaTeX math ($/$$ dollars mode, throwOnError:false) via markdown-it-texmath + markdown-it-footnote. katex.min.css + woff2 fonts vendored into media/katex/ (offline-first), served on all three asset surfaces. New code path-safe for Windows. Spec: docs/specs/OMD-012-katex-footnotes.md; plan: docs/plans/2026-07-10-omd-012-katex-footnotes.md. Executed by Codex CLI, reviewed/verified/committed by Claude on host.
acceptance_criteria: $...$ inline and $$...$$ block math render via server-side KaTeX; plain $5 text and escaped \$ stay literal; invalid TeX never crashes the pipeline.
Footnotes ([^1] refs, definitions, backrefs) render and match the theme in light+dark.
media/katex/** (css + woff2 only) built by esbuild, staged, served at /assets/katex/** with woff2 content type and traversal-safe whitelist, linked in all three RenderAssets surfaces.
Full test suite passes incl. new unit tests; vsix contains media/katex/**; manual offline check passes in browser + webview.
No new POSIX path assumptions (diff reviewed).

features.title: Windows compatibility audit + CI test matrix
features.description: Audit ALL existing code for POSIX path assumptions (mirror-path temp files, preview server, cleanup, asset staging) and fix; add windows-latest to the CI test matrix. Agreed during OMD-012 brainstorming (2026-07-10). Needs a spec before starting.
acceptance_criteria: Audit findings documented; every POSIX-only path/sep assumption fixed or justified.
CI runs the unit test suite on windows-latest and it passes.
Manual or integration confirmation that browser + webview preview work on Windows.

features.title: Extension settings: theme, server port, auto-refresh toggle
features.description: User-facing settings: preview theme (light/dark/auto), preview server port, auto-refresh on/off. Agreed during OMD-012 brainstorming (2026-07-10). Needs a spec before starting.
acceptance_criteria: Settings appear under the extension's configuration section with sane defaults matching current behavior.
Each setting takes effect without reloading VS Code (or documents when a reload is needed).
Tests cover the render/server behavior changes driven by each setting.

features.title: Marketplace listing overhaul (demo GIF + metadata + comparison table)
features.description: Growth work (2026-07-13): demo GIF recorded via standalone PreviewServer + Playwright + Pillow (docs/demo.gif, embedded in README); package.json listing metadata sharpened (search-oriented description, categories Visualization+Other, 15 keywords, galleryBanner #0D47A1 dark); README gets marketplace/Open VSX badges and a 'Why not the built-in preview?' comparison table. Goes live on the marketplace at the next release (README/metadata ship with the vsix).
acceptance_criteria: docs/demo.gif exists (<300 KB), embedded at the top of README, excluded from the vsix via .vscodeignore docs/**.
package.json has Visualization category, >=10 search keywords, galleryBanner, and a description containing 'markdown preview' search terms.
README has version/installs/rating badges and a built-in-preview comparison table.
npm run test:unit passes and vsce accepts the manifest (vsce ls).

features.title: Activation cleanup can match foreign extensions by ID prefix
features.description: Found during the OMD-013 audit (2026-07-13): activation cleanup selects sibling extension dirs with entry.startsWith(extensionId), so an extension whose ID shares the prefix (e.g. publisher.openmd-pro) would get its .temp dir deleted. Require the ID to be followed by a version delimiter and validate the versioned dir form. Needs a spec before starting.
acceptance_criteria: Cleanup only touches directories matching <extensionId>-<semver> exactly.
Unit test covers the prefix-collision case (extracted helper, no vscode imports).
Existing cleanup behavior for our own old versions is unchanged.

features.title: Code block theme mismatch: hljs css tied to OS scheme + double box strands copy button
features.description: Human-reported (2026-07-13, screenshots): with OS light + page toggled dark, code blocks rendered as white boxes (hljs stylesheets were gated on prefers-color-scheme, ignoring the in-page data-theme toggle), and hljs themes paint code.hljs with their own background+padding, drawing a second box inside the pre so the copy button (absolutely positioned in the pre) appeared stranded outside the visible code box. Fix: pre code.hljs { background: transparent; padding: 0 } so the pre is the single box, and setTheme() now switches the two hljs <link>s via media="all"/"not all" (link.disabled toggling permanently detached the sheet in Chromium — found during browser verification).
acceptance_criteria: Toggling light/dark/auto switches hljs syntax colors and code-block background together, repeatedly, without OS theme involvement beyond auto mode.
Code block renders as a single box; copy button sits inside it top-right.
Copy button still copies the code text.
Unit tests cover the css override and the media-attribute switching markup.

features.title: Marketplace SEO: searchable displayName + expanded keywords
features.description: Growth follow-on to OMD-016 (2026-07-20): the displayName was the bare brand 'OpenMD' (zero search weight), and keywords omitted long-tail terms OpenMD genuinely wins on. Changed displayName to 'OpenMD — Markdown Preview in Browser' (VS Code weights displayName heavily in search; the tagline follows the GitLens/Prettier convention, not keyword stuffing) and added 8 keywords: pdf, print (browser print→PDF, real for this ext), readme, documentation, technical writing, cursor, windsurf, vscodium (Open VSX reach). Metadata-only; goes live on the marketplace at the next release.
acceptance_criteria: package.json displayName contains 'Markdown Preview' search terms while keeping 'OpenMD' as the lead word.
keywords include pdf, print, readme, documentation, technical writing, cursor, windsurf, vscodium; total <= 30.
package.json is valid JSON and vsce accepts the manifest (vsce ls).
npm run test:unit passes.

features.title: Export to self-contained HTML + Export to PDF (via browser print)
features.description: Growth feature (2026-07-20, design approved by owner). Right-click a .md file to (a) Export to HTML — one fully self-contained .html written next to the source (all assets inlined; Mermaid JS only if diagrams present, KaTeX css/fonts only if math present; no live-reload; adds @media print styles), meant to be sent to people with no VS Code/internet; and (b) Export to PDF — open the doc in the real browser with ?print=1 so the page auto-triggers the print dialog after Mermaid/hljs render, user picks Save as PDF (no PDF engine bundled, keeps the tiny+offline identity). Spec: docs/specs/OMD-020-export-html-pdf.md. Execution to be delegated to Codex CLI; Claude reviews/verifies/commits.
acceptance_criteria: generateStandaloneHtml produces HTML with no http/https/localhost//assets/ references; Mermaid JS inlined iff a mermaid block is present; KaTeX css/fonts inlined iff math is present; no live-reload script; @media print block present.
openmd.exportHtml writes <basename>.html next to the source and offers Reveal/Open; openmd.exportPdf opens the browser preview with ?print=1 and the page calls window.print() after render.
Both commands appear in explorer/editor context menus and the command palette for .md files; all path handling uses src/paths.ts helpers.

npm run compile and full npm run test:unit pass (new pure unit tests for the standalone builder + print-trigger markup); host/browser check: an exported HTML with Mermaid+math renders offline and Export to PDF shows the print dialog with diagrams rendered.
